name: Purple Sprouting
image_alt: Purple-tinged broccoli shoots in late winter garden
description: An overwintering broccoli planted in summer and harvested in late winter or early spring. Produces masses of purple-tinged shoots with a sweet, almost nutty flavor that is distinctly different from standard broccoli.
history: A traditional British and Italian garden vegetable, grown for centuries to provide fresh greens in late winter when almost nothing else is available. It fills the 'hungry gap' — the period before spring crops are ready.
seasonal_quirks: Must be planted in summer to overwinter and produce in late winter/spring. Needs cold exposure to trigger flowering. Purple color intensifies in cold. The long season demands patience but the late-winter harvest is uniquely rewarding.
harvest_cues: Cut shoots when 4–6 inches long, before flowers open. The whole shoot including leaves and stem is edible. Harvest frequently to encourage continuous production.
variety_notes: The purple color fades to green when cooked. Flavor is sweeter and less sulfurous than summer broccoli. One of the most satisfying crops for dedicated gardeners willing to plan months ahead.
culinary_uses: sautéed with garlic and olive oil
preservation: Best eaten fresh. Blanch and freeze if needed.
